treatment: Retained title, eight named authors, issuing agency and region, FHP report number, March 2022 revision, and the statement that this revision replaces earlier versions.
reason: Required source identity, scope, and attribution.

treatment: Used only manually paraphrased text eligible as an official federal work. Made no blanket rights claim for visual, form, table, application, or cited third-party components.
reason: The official agency report supports a federal-text presumption, but embedded-component rights still require separate review.

treatment: Retained the Pacific Southwest Region, California, National Forest System recreation-site, road, facility, and forest-management context. Prevented use as a universal arboriculture standard.
reason: Local program scope controls interpretation of every later rating and example.

treatment: Retained that all failures cannot be predicted and structured professional judgment can reduce but not eliminate injury or property risk.
reason: This is the report's central uncertainty and anti-certification boundary.

treatment: Retained distinctions among defect, failure, target, loss, hazard, and administrative hazard-rating system. Corrected the training surface so a defect alone is not treated as a complete hazard statement.
reason: Separating tree condition from target consequence prevents category errors.

treatment: Retained dependence on public use, failure history, insects, disease, fire, weather, site condition, resources, and program priorities. Preserved post-storm, post-fire, and reopening context without teaching one universal interval.
reason: Inspection frequency is a documented local program decision, not a safety guarantee.

treatment: Retained trained inspection, multiple perspectives and distances, useful lighting, local history, professional judgment, and documentation of positive and negative observations.
reason: These principles improve evidence quality without becoming a lay clearance checklist.

treatment: Did not reproduce cutting, drilling, coring, sounding, tomographic, resistograph, axe, or other invasive testing procedures. Retained only the warning that wounding can cause further damage and advanced investigation belongs to qualified professionals.
reason: Procedures can injure a tree and require skill, context, and interpretation.

treatment: Retained reach, target type, occupancy, exposure duration and frequency, likely consequence, part size, distance, direction, slope, mobility, and congregation as contextual factors.
reason: Target exposure is half of the report's hazard framework and must remain separate from defect probability.

treatment: Omitted all tree-height multipliers, default radii, treatment-area distances, examples, regulatory distances, and depicted zones. Retained only that reach is estimated and can change with slope, breakage, obstacles, wind, and interacting trees.
reason: Region 5 public-land conventions are not guarantees, anchor-site rules, or human-suspension clearances.

treatment: Retained multiple crack origins, possible association with buckling or decay, importance of location, through-cracking, paired cracks, lean, and contact with other defects. Excluded sizes and rating categories.
reason: Cracks are evidence requiring context, not one diagnosis or score.

treatment: Retained wood connection, included bark, replacement leaders, prior top injury, epicormic growth, and interaction with cracks, decay, cankers, or loading history. Excluded rating categories and host exceptions.
reason: Architecture and history are informative only with whole-tree professional interpretation.

treatment: Retained that crown vigor may not reveal internal decay; possible indicators; and dependence on location, extent, remaining sound tissue, cavities, horizontal orientation, lean, cracks, and unions. Excluded the one-third rule and all measurements.
reason: A Region 5 decay-shell rule is not universal and cannot calculate anchor capacity.

treatment: Retained that a fruiting body can indicate decay but meaning depends on fungus, host, number, location, associated condition, and local history. Excluded every fungus-host threshold and category.
reason: Avoids converting a California specialist list into a universal photo checklist.

treatment: Retained dead-cambium or exposed-sapwood concept, possible fungal, insect, weather, fire, or mechanical origins, underlying decay, expansion, and connection to cracks, lean, or other defects. Excluded species, circumference, and disease thresholds.
reason: General mechanism is useful; categorical California ratings are not portable.

treatment: Retained the near-impossibility of predicting dead-tree failure timing, relevant biological and weather factors, target reach, lodged parts, decay, and prior breakage. Excluded species, length, diameter, percentage, and rating rules.
reason: Preserves the valid uncertainty without creating branch-approval thresholds.

treatment: Retained interaction with stress, fire, disease, mortality, entry of decay organisms, and possible indicators with multiple causes. Excluded insect names, host lists, colors, percentages, circumference rules, and ratings.
reason: Local biological expertise is required; a digest should not diagnose infestation.

treatment: Retained hidden-root uncertainty; physical and biological categories; compaction, erosion, fire, saturation, flooding, construction, equipment and traffic; root lifting, soil cracks and mounding; crown and basal clues; and nearby failures. Excluded disease, host, distance, percentage, and rating rules.
reason: Root and soil context are high-value inspection knowledge but not a lay diagnosis.

treatment: Retained longstanding compensated versus recent or changing lean, root and soil associations, bows, crown imbalance, old injury, poor pruning, nearby removals, and recurrent weather. Explicitly rejected treating corrected growth as stability or capacity certification.
reason: Temporal change is informative, but the source's stable-lean language is too strong for anchor transfer.

treatment: Retained that connected defects can increase failure concern and that merely counting unrelated defects is not equivalent. Omitted arithmetic scoring.
reason: Interaction is more faithful than a decontextualized defect count.

treatment: Did not reproduce point values, score arithmetic, score bands, suggested actions, target examples, defect categories, or the raster rating table. Retained only that Region 5 combines target and detectable-defect information for prioritization.
reason: An administrative score is neither a physical capacity measurement nor a portable decision rule.

treatment: Excluded target relocation, felling, removal, topping, pruning, closures, warnings, cabling, bracing, support poles, stump treatment, wound treatment, and every operational mitigation instruction.
reason: The accepted corpus scope is inspection knowledge, not tree work or construction.

treatment: Retained change detection, post-weather review, observation records, inspection history, trend tracking, and learning from later failures. Excluded mapping codes, action workflow, forms, and legal-administration detail.
reason: A record trail improves longitudinal interpretation without becoming an agency form drill.

source_region: Forest Health Protection and arborist assistance
treatment: Retained referral to Forest Health Protection specialists, a certified arborist when local expertise is absent for large-hardwood work, and professional arborists for specialized actions. Added a clearly labeled corpus gate requiring separate rigging or structural assessment for any proposed load.
reason: Questions beyond competence should be referred; arboricultural expertise does not itself certify a load path.

treatment: Retained only scale, disturbance context, resource limits, and unavoidable over- and under-inclusion in forecasting. Excluded treatment areas, probability thresholds, marking, designation, felling, removal, contracting, and streamlined abatement steps.
reason: The uncertainty generalizes within hazard assessment; the abatement workflow does not belong in this corpus.

treatment: Did not copy reference annotations, titles as training knowledge, claims from cited works, or external links. Used citations only to audit that the report relies on third-party and other-region material.
reason: Cited sources were not independently reviewed or cleared and would introduce identifier trivia.

source_region: Appendices 1-3: evaluation form, TATUM quick list, and Survey123 application
treatment: Excluded the form, map and location codes, scoring instructions, quick-list duplication, suggested-action table, account and application workflow, screenshot, and all expressive layouts.
reason: Administrative artifacts and repeated ratings add low-value memorization and component-rights risk.

source_region: Appendices 4-5: catastrophic abatement and failure zone
treatment: Excluded treatment-area design, mortality and failure thresholds, numerical distances, marking and cutting decisions, OSHA and other-region comparisons, contractor workflow, and operational examples. Retained only uncertainty already summarized in record 025.
reason: These appendices govern Region 5 abatement, not anchor location, construction, rigging, or human suspension.

source_region: All figures, table, cover art, forms, screenshot, and embedded graphics
treatment: Audited six numbered figures, the raster Table 1, cover graphic, appendix form graphics, quick-list graphics, and Survey123 screenshot. No photograph, diagram, raster, vector layout, screenshot, or source table was fetched separately, retained, traced, or reproduced.
reason: No blanket federal-rights assumption is made for embedded components, and none is needed for the textual digest.

treatment: Used the exact official USDA Forest Service PDF for manual review and recorded URL, headers, byte length, SHA-256, PDF metadata, page count, and visual-object audit. The binary is not retained and is not direct training.
reason: Integrity and reproducibility are preserved without storing a large mixed-component source artifact.
